headline: Teenage Engineering adds lo-fi mode, USB audio, and more to its KO II sampler
summary: Teenage Engineering's EP-133 KO II sampler receives a major OS 2.5 update. The update introduces audio over USB and selectable lo-fi sample rates. New features also include sample reverse, an arpeggiator, and extended sample length.

headline: Margaret Atwood says the problem with AI is ‘garbage in, garbage out’
summary: Author Margaret Atwood critiqued AI during a literary festival, stating its output relies on input quality. She highlighted the 'garbage in, garbage out' principle regarding AI's limitations. Atwood mentioned her own experiments with AI, reinforcing her skeptical view.

headline: Apple wants permission to buy memory from a blacklisted Chinese supplier
summary: Apple is seeking US government approval to purchase RAM chips from CXMT, a blacklisted Chinese firm. This move aims to alleviate supply chain pressures amid soaring memory prices. CXMT was previously blacklisted by the Pentagon due to alleged ties with the People's Liberation Army.

headline: The Guardian’s Kai Wright refuses to buy a new phone
summary: Peabody Award-winning journalist Kai Wright continues to refuse purchasing a new phone. Wright, co-host of 'Stateside with Kai and Carter,' is known for his distinct perspectives. His stance highlights a personal choice against constant tech upgrades.

headline: The Invincible VS Devs Would Love a Mortal Kombat Crossover
summary: Invincible VS developers expressed strong interest in collaborating with other IPs and genres. They specifically welcomed the possibility of a Mortal Kombat crossover. This could potentially introduce iconic Mortal Kombat characters into their game.

headline: GTA 6 Scalpers Are Finding Buyers on eBay Despite Rockstar's Commitment to a Digital Release
summary: Scalpers are successfully selling GTA 6 pre-orders on eBay for inflated prices. This occurs despite Rockstar Games confirming a digital-only release for the game. Fans are paying extra without a clear advantage for these 'pre-order' copies.

headline: Street Fighter 6 Devs Have No Current Plans to Make Any Major Adjustments to System Mechanics | Evo 2026
summary: Street Fighter 6 developers stated they have no immediate plans for major system mechanic adjustments. Director Nakayama and Producer Matsumoto shared this update at Evo 2026. They discussed the game's current balance and future content plans.

headline: Supergirl Director Credits James Gunn for Helping Choose Song for Much-Discussed Slow-Motion Fight Scene
summary: Director Craig Gillespie credited DC Studios co-CEO James Gunn for a key creative decision on Supergirl. Gunn helped select the song for a highly discussed slow-motion fight scene. The music choice for the scene sparked significant social media attention.

headline: Clarke steps down as Scotland boss after World Cup exit
summary: Steve Clarke has resigned from his position as Scotland's head coach. His decision follows the national team's group stage exit from the World Cup. Clarke's departure marks the end of his tenure after a disappointing tournament performance.

headline: 'Every person saved is a miracle': Families call to trapped loved ones in region devastated by Venezuela quakes
summary: Families desperately search for trapped loved ones in regions devastated by Venezuela quakes. They maintain vigils at collapsed buildings, facing immense challenges with heavy debris. Rescue workers describe every person saved as a miracle amidst the impossible conditions.

headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
summary: The US and Iran have exchanged retaliatory strikes, accusing each other of ceasefire violations. Iran claims to have targeted US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain. This escalation follows earlier US strikes across Iran.

headline: Harry reconsiders bringing Meghan and children on UK trip
summary: Prince Harry is reportedly reconsidering bringing Meghan and their children on an upcoming UK trip. This reconsideration follows the rejection of his request for police protection. The family's planned visit to the UK is now uncertain.
